overview: Since witest28-1 consists of exactly one component with no external dependencies, the entire implementation is completed in a single phase. There are no upstream services to build first, no API contracts to negotiate between components, and no data layers to establish — the React/TypeScript SPA is fully self-contained and served directly from an Nginx container.

phases.name: Foundation & Delivery
phases.description: The sole component of the system — a static React/TypeScript SPA served via Nginx — has no dependencies and constitutes the entire application. It is built and deployed in this single phase.

components.name: hello-world-web
responsibilities: Render a semantically correct, accessible 'Hello World' greeting page at the root path '/'.
Serve a /health endpoint returning HTTP 200 with JSON body {"status":"ok"} via Nginx configuration.
Handle undefined routes by rendering a React 404 page via React Router's catch-all route, avoiding 5xx errors.
Enforce security response headers (X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy) and suppress server version disclosure via server_tokens off.
Produce a minified, self-contained static bundle (target well under 500 KB) using Vite's production build pipeline.
components.architectureContext: This is the only component in the system. It is accessed directly by end users via a browser over HTTP/HTTPS (HTTPS termination handled at the ingress/load-balancer layer). Nginx serves the pre-built Vite dist artefacts and handles the /health route natively — no backend, database, or inter-service communication is involved.
keyConsiderations: The Nginx error_page 404 directive routes unknown static assets to index.html, while React Router's wildcard route ('*') renders the NotFoundPage component — both layers must cooperate to avoid a mismatch between HTTP status codes and visual feedback.
server_tokens off must be verified in the final Docker image using curl -I to confirm the 'Server' header does not leak the Nginx version, satisfying requirement 11.
WCAG 2.1 Level A contrast compliance (#1a1a1a on #ffffff yields ~19:1 contrast ratio) should be validated with an automated tool (e.g., axe-core or Lighthouse) as part of the CI pipeline.

The multi-stage Dockerfile (Node 20 Alpine builder → Nginx 1.25 Alpine runtime) must be tested for correct COPY of the dist/ directory; a misconfigured path will result in a blank page with no build-time error.

Mobile viewport correctness (<meta name='viewport' content='width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0'>) and cross-browser rendering (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge) should be validated using a browser compatibility test or BrowserStack before marking the component complete.
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: hello-world-web
  description: Static Hello World web application served via Nginx.
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Hello World page
      description: Returns the Hello World HTML page.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Successful HTML page response.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      description: Returns 200 OK when the server is healthy.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Server is healthy.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /{path}:
    get:
      summary: Catch-all for undefined routes
      parameters:
        - name: path
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '404':
          description: Route not found.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
